stories.headline: Russia pummels Ukraine with drone and missile strikes, killing at least 12
stories.summary: Missile and drone attacks on the port city of Odesa killed six people, with other fatalities recorded in Kyiv and DniproRussian strikes killed at least 12 people in Ukraine, local authorities have said, after Moscow pummelled its neighbour in overnight attacks.Missile and drone attacks on the southern port city of Odesa killed six people, the head of the city’s military administration, Sergiy Lysak, wrote on Telegram on Thursday. Continue reading

stories.headline: Ukraine: Deadly Russian strikes on Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro leave 12 dead
stories.summary: Russian strikes on Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro killed at least 12 people, including a child, as fighting and failed ceasefire efforts drag on in mid-April.

stories.headline: Russian attacks on Ukraine kill 14, including a child, authorities say
stories.summary: Deadly attacks hit Kyiv, Odesa, and Dnipro, while Russia says two children killed in Ukrainian attacks.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Pakistan in push for new round of US-Iran peace negotiations
stories.summary: At least 2,167 people have been killed and more than 7,000 injured in Israeli attacks on Lebanon since March 2.

stories.headline: Australia: Refinery fire raises fuel supply concerns amid Iran war
stories.summary: Fire at Viva Energy's Geelong refinery, which supplies 10% of Australia’s fuel, sparks supply fears as Iran war keeps global prices elevated.

stories.headline: Wave of Russian drone and missile attacks kill 15 in Ukraine
stories.summary: Meanwhile, a Ukrainian drone attack killed two children in Russia, officials say.

stories.headline: The pope versus the president: how Leo became Trump’s fearless foe
stories.summary: As he steps up his criticism of the Iran war, the pontiff’s American background has given him a role in US politics unlike any predecessor

stories.headline: 3 Killed in Boat Strike in the Pacific, Pentagon Says
stories.summary: It was the third such attack by the U.S. military in three days, and the 51st attack in a campaign against people who the United States has accused of smuggling drugs.

stories.headline: How Trump’s Clash With Pope Leo Turned Into a Fight Over Theology
stories.summary: When President Trump and G.O.P. leaders denounced the pope’s comments about the U.S. attack on Iran, they touched off a religious debate over what is and isn’t a “just war.”

stories.headline: US military kills three in new Eastern Pacific boat strike
stories.summary: The attack is the latest in a string of killings by the United States that rights groups say are 'unlawful'.

stories.headline: How the Strait of Hormuz will change Iran’s regime
stories.summary: A system fixated on building up military deterrents has discovered its most important point of leverage over the world

stories.headline: Ceasefire with Israel expected ‘soon’, say Lebanese officials
stories.summary: Deal to halt war between Israeli and Hizbollah could boost efforts to reach a peace accord between the US and Iran

stories.headline: EasyJet Shares Slump Amid Warning of Loss Driven By Iran War
stories.summary: EasyJet Plc shares fell the most in almost four years after the UK budget airline warned of a loss in the first half year, caused by the disruption from the Iran war.

stories.headline: China’s Economy Rides Out War as Growth Unexpectedly Touches 5%
stories.summary: China’s economic growth rebounded more than expected in the first quarter, suggesting limited spillovers so far from the war in Iran but revealing few signs of turnaround in weak consumer spending.

stories.headline: CATL Overtakes PetroChina in Market Value as Green Bets Ramp Up
stories.summary: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. has once again overtaken oil giant PetroChina Co. in market value, as investors pile into the battery maker on war-driven optimism over green energy.

stories.headline: Japan pledges $10bn to help Asian countries deal with oil crisis
stories.summary: The aid is roughly equivalent to a year's worth of crude oil imports by Asean countries.

stories.headline: Taiwan overtakes UK in stock market value on AI chip boom
stories.summary: Crossover comes as chipmaker TSMC reports record first-quarter profit

stories.headline: Indian LNG Importers Scoop Up Spot Shipments After Prices Recede
stories.summary: India’s liquefied natural gas importers have accelerated purchases from the spot market, taking advantage of a recent dip in prices, as the country looks to ease a supply crunch triggered by the war in the Middle East.

stories.headline: How South Korea plans to use the Iran crisis to spur a renewables revolution
stories.summary: Energy crisis unfolding in Middle East has added political urgency, and more funding, to transform South Korea’s solar industryIn Guyang-ri, a farming village of 70 households about 90 minutes south-east of Seoul, people gather for communal free lunches six days a week. stories.headline: Won't Be Seeing Three ECB Rate Hikes in 2026, BlackRock Says
stories.summary: James Turner, BlackRock global fixed income EMEA head, discusses the European economy and bond space in light of the current geopolitical situation in the Middle East and its impact on supply chains. "We have to remember that it's very much a supply-side shock," Turner tells Bloomberg Television. stories.headline: Singapore Rejects US Claims in Manufacturing, Forced Labor Probe
stories.summary: Singapore’s business lobby has joined the government in pushing back against a sweeping US trade probe that alleges manufacturing overcapacity and failures to enforce bans on imports linked to forced labor.

stories.headline: Abu Dhabi Fund Led by Crown Prince Inks $2.3 Billion Jordan Deal
stories.summary: A newly created wealth fund overseen by Abu Dhabi’s crown prince has signed a $2.3 billion pact for a railway project in Jordan, highlighting the role the entity is likely to play in advancing the emirate’s strategic relationships.

stories.headline: Lawyer John Eastman Disbarred for Efforts to Overturn 2020 Election
stories.summary: The California Supreme Court upheld a lower court’s decision that said Mr. Eastman, had violated the rules of professional ethics.

stories.headline: DeSantis Delays Redistricting Special Session and Adds a Vaccine Bill
stories.summary: The Florida governor is expected to propose a congressional map that could result in up to five new Republican-leaning seats. Some in the party fear it could backfire.

stories.headline: Muslims the target? Fury as millions lose voting rights in India’s Bengal
stories.summary: Key eastern state heads to polls under the shadow of controversial voter list revision, which many say excludes Muslims.

stories.headline: Suzuki Motor, after India success, sets sights on 10% of African market
Seeking rest and relaxation? Bulgaria taps into the wellness trend as spa tourism rises 15%
stories.summary: Bulgaria is positioning itself as a spa destination, with a year-round tourism strategy also focused on health, food, mountain, pilgrimage and luxury travel.

stories.headline: 'Discussions are being had' on second round of Iran talks in Pakistan, White House says
stories.summary: Negotiators are focusing on three key points that derailed last weekend's talks, including Iran's nuclear programme, the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for wartime damages.

stories.headline: North Korea rapidly expanding nuclear weapons capability, UN watchdog warns
stories.summary: Pyongyang making ‘very serious’ progress on producing weapons, with rapid rise in activity at main nuclear complexNorth Korea has made “very serious” progress in its ability to produce more nuclear weapons, the head of the UN’s nuclear watchdog has said, in another sign that the regime is seeking to use its nuclear arsenal to ensure its survival.North Korea is thought to have assembled about 50 nuclear warheads, although some experts are sceptica

stories.headline: North Korea showing 'serious increase' in ability to make nuclear weapons, IAEA says
stories.summary: North Korea, which conducted its first nuclear test in 2006, is under rafts of UN sanctions for its banned weapons programmes.

stories.headline: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.summary: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.headline: Nine killed in second Turkish school shooting in two days
stories.summary: Eight students and one teacher died in the attack, according to Interior Minister Mustafa Cifci.

stories.headline: Senate Republicans Again Block Bid to Limit Trump’s Iran War Powers
stories.summary: For the fourth time since the war began, G.O.P. senators successfully fended off an effort to constrain the president. But there were signs of growing unease among Republicans.

stories.headline: Flotilla carrying activists and aid for Palestinians in Gaza sets sail from Spain
stories.summary: Organisers of the Global Sumud Flotilla say that more than 70 boats and 1,000 people from around the world will participate.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Italy pledge to work together to boost defence and drone production
stories.summary: Zelenskyy is pressing European countries to join the PURL programme, launched last year, that allows Ukraine to receive US equipment financed by European countries.

stories.headline: 120,000 drones and more missiles: Allies double down on Ukraine aid in Berlin
stories.summary: Ukraine’s Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov emphasised that Kyiv is pushing to scale up domestic drone production and accelerate deliveries to the frontline.

stories.headline: Magyar calls for fast handover of power in talks with Hungary's president
stories.summary: Péter Magyar, the man who ended Viktor Orbán's 16 years of continuous rule, wants the new parliament to convene in early May.

stories.headline: ECB Officials Are Leaning Toward April Rate Hold
stories.summary: Policymakers at the European Central Bank are leaning toward keeping interest rates unchanged this month, postponing their verdict on whether the fallout of the Iran war warrants a response, according to people familiar with the debate.

stories.headline: Evergrande Liquidators Said to Pick State-Owned Tourism Firm for Exclusive Sale Talks
stories.summary: China Evergrande Group’s liquidators have selected Guangdong Provincial Tourism Holdings Co. to enter exclusive talks to acquire a majority stake in the developer’s property management unit, people familiar with the situation said, as they try to claw back some money from one of the country’s most significant corporate collapses.

stories.headline: White House touts refunds, as most Americans say taxes are too high
stories.summary: The president hopes voters will focus on the benefit of “No Tax on Tips” and other breaks, but economists say higher gas prices will negate most of those benefits.

stories.headline: Trump says China has agreed not to send weapons to Iran
stories.summary: The president made the claim, which Beijing has yet to confirm, amid anger in China that its vessels could be caught up in a U.S. blockade targeting Iranian ports.

stories.headline: El Salvador publishes law allowing life sentences for minors as young as 12
stories.summary: The new sentencing measure comes as President Nayib Bukele oversees a four-year-long state of emergency against crime.

stories.headline: Peru’s ballot count drags on amid frustration with presidential election
stories.summary: The turbulent election has prompted fraud claims by candidates and frustration with the country's chaotic politics.

stories.headline: Péter Magyar's landslide victory in Hungary - what's the impact on Brussels?
stories.summary: In this new edition of The Ring, broadcast from the European Parliament in Brussels, MEPs Daniel Freund (Greens) and Sander Smit (ECR) debate the outcome of the Hungarian election and what it means for Europe.

stories.headline: Panicked scenes after school shooting in Turkey leaves at least nine dead
stories.summary: A 14-year-old armed with five guns opened fire at a Turkish school on Wednesday, killing at least nine people, wounding 13 others, the interior minister said.

stories.headline: Hungary’s Péter Magyar says new government could take power at beginning of May
stories.summary: Magyar has called on Orbán's government to act as a caretaker in its final weeks and not to make any decisions that could threaten Hungary's interests or impede the incoming government's work.

stories.headline: Kanye West postpones France gig following UK ban
stories.summary: It comes a week after the UK government announced it would block the rapper from entering the country.

stories.headline: Watch: Vance heckled as he chides Pope
stories.summary: The US Vice-President was speaking at a Turning Point USA event, where he said Pope Leo should "be careful when he talks about matters of theology."

stories.headline: SoftBank Pays Record 8.5% on Part of $3.6 Billion Bond Sale
stories.summary: SoftBank Group Corp. agreed to pay its highest interest rate on dollar notes as part of a $3.6 billion fundraising, underscoring concerns about the Japanese conglomerate’s large-scale investments in artificial intelligence.

stories.headline: Sigenergy Jumps 100% in Hong Kong Debut After $562 Million IPO
stories.summary: Shares of Sigenergy Technology Co., a Chinese maker of energy storage equipment, jumped in their Hong Kong trading debut Thursday after the company raised HK$4.4 billion ($562 million) in an initial public offering, a sign of investor demand in an industry swept up in a frenzied rally.

stories.headline: Trump’s ‘Triumphal Arch’ Draws Backlash, Even From an Expert Who Proposed It
stories.summary: The story of how President Trump quadrupled the size of the original proposal for the arch follows a now-familiar pattern.

stories.headline: Trump administration pushes nations to sign ‘trade over aid’ declaration
stories.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio called it an opportunity to use the U.N. system to “promote America First values,” according to a cable reviewed by The Post.

stories.headline: Brazil’s police open a probe into presidential candidate Flavio Bolsonaro
stories.summary: Investigation centres on whether Bolsonaro committed defamation by linking Lula to crimes like drug trafficking.

stories.headline: Korean Air Up on Earnings Surprise Despite Fuel Fears
stories.summary: Korean Air Lines Co. shares climbed after South Korea’s largest flag carrier delivered a first-quarter earnings surprise, defying mounting concerns about jet fuel shortages and surging costs amid the Middle East crisis.

stories.headline: Founder of China's Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud
stories.summary: Evergrande was once China's biggest real estate firm, with a stock market valuation of more than $50bn.

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan mix charity and business on Australia visit
stories.summary: There are questions over whether local taxpayers are picking up any of the bill for policing the visit.

stories.headline: Bongbong Marcos fights ill-health rumours with star jumps
stories.summary: The Philippine President challenged anyone questioning his fitness to join him in the gym.

stories.headline: America’s corporate boards are under siege
stories.summary: Activist shareholders and regulatory changes are undermining their control

stories.headline: What happened when Trump told a city to forget DEI or lose federal money
stories.summary: The debate in Fort Worth echoes a dilemma that local leaders are grappling with around the country: End diversity programs or risk losing all federal funding.

stories.headline: How Trump’s Jesus-like image and feud with the Pope are sparking backlash
stories.summary: BBC's Sarah Smith looks at how the president's most ardent supporters are reacting to his latest social media posts.

stories.headline: Israelis war-weary but most oppose Iran ceasefire, poll suggests
stories.summary: The vast majority did not believe Hezbollah or Iran had been severely weakened by weeks of Israeli and US attacks, according to the poll.

stories.headline: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.headline: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
stories.summary: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.

stories.headline: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism
stories.summary: Italy's prime minister and the US president are close allies, but Trump has refused to apologise to the "very weak" Pope Leo XIV.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Ceasefire or no ceasefire, the Middle East's reshuffling is not yet done
stories.summary: Both sides have reason to end the war but share no common ground.

stories.headline: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.summary: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.
